person.name: Robert de Ogle
person.alternateName: Sir Robert Angle of Bothal and Ogle
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Sir Robert Ogle is the son of Robert Ogle (after 1331-Nov 1355) and Joan Hepple. He may have been born at Callerton on 08 December 1353. Ogle (1902), agrees with the month, day and location, but says he was born in 1351 ... adding that he was baptized in Ponteland.

Robert was knighted before 12 Richard II (c. 1387). According to Froissart, Ogle prepared for the Battle of Otterburn at Newcastle with Sir Ralph Lumley, Sir Matthew Redman, captain of Berwick, Sir Thomas Grey, Sir Thomas Holton, Sir John Felton, Sir John Lilleburn, Sir Thomas Abingdon, Baron Hilton, and Sir John Coppledike; but was apparently taken prisoner.

He was already married to Joan de Heaton, by the time he was twenty-one. As daughter and co-heir of Sir Alexander de Heton, Knt., Joan's property went to Richard in the 12th year of Richard II's reign (c. 1389).

Because his father died prematurely in 1355, Robert became his grandfather's heir in 1363. Part of his inheritance included Bothal castle and manor, but it was the home of his step-grandmother, Blanche Bertram. Helen Bertram had the reversion of Bothal, only after Blanche died.

Robert's death in October of 1409/11, triggered an inheritance battle over Bothal Castle between his sons: heir Robert (1379 -1436), and the distinguished John Bertram ... who not only had the legal claim, but actually lived there.

«b»Other Property«/b»
Hephall manor
Town of Lour-bottil.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Ogle於1351年12月8日出生在英國諾圖波藍德卡萊頓時，他的父親Robert de Ogle vp19歲，母親Helen Bertram Baroness of Bothall10歲。他於1372年之前與Joan Heton結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1409年10月31日在英國諾圖波藍德奧格爾 卡素享年57歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德可哈姆聖 安德也斯 邱奇。

meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ogle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Northumberland and Durham): from Ogle in Whalton, Northumberland. The placename might derive from the Old English personal name Ocga + Old English hyll ‘hill’. This surname has been established in Ireland since the mid 17th century.

variants.name: Ogles
meanings: English (southeastern): variant of Ogle , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Ogle was born on 8 December 1351, in Callerton, Northumberland, England, his father, Robert de Ogle vp, was 19 and his mother, Helen Bertram Baroness of Bothall, was 10. He married Joan Heton before 1372. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died about 31 October 1409, in Ogle Castle, Northumberland, England, at the age of 57, and was buried in St Andrew's Church, Hexham, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Joan Heton avant 1372. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé environ 31 octobre 1409, à Ogle Castle, Northumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterré à St Andrew's Church, Hexham, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Ogle(이)가 1351년 12월 8일 영국 노르스벌랜드 캘레르턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Ogle vp(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Helen Bertram Baroness of Bothall(은)는 10세였다. 그는 1372년 이전 Joan Heton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1409년 10월 31일 영국 노르스벌랜드 오글 카스틀에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 헤스함 스트 앤드루'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Joan Heton antes 1372. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Ogle родился 8 декабря 1351 г. в местности каллиртон, нортумбирланд, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert de Ogle vp было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Helen Bertram Baroness of Bothall, 10. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Heton перед 1372 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер около 31 октября 1409 г. в местности огл кастл, нортумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 57 лет и был похоронен в местности ст андрю'с чурч, хексхам, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания.
